stance: Risk-aware flexi-cap: keep the high-conviction quality/cyclical core, tilt toward names with strong/rising analyst revisions, and prune the clearly-falling-revision laggards while adding liquid large/mid ballast to temper a hot beta.
experience_note: I am watching whether the analyst-revision tilt actually adds information ratio over the next 1-3 months: the names I am adding/keeping for strong revisions (ONGC, LICI, MUTHOOTFIN, COFORGE, GUJGASLTD, GLAND, EQUITASBNK) should, as a basket, outperform the falling-revision names I am exiting (ADANIPORTS, HINDPETRO, LT, SRF, SUMICHEM, MFSL). If that revision spread does NOT show up - i.e. the exited basket holds up as well as the added basket over a fair common window - then ARM added no transfer here and I am over-trusting a weak (IC~0.05) signal; I would cut the tilt and revert toward the liquid-core baseline. I am also watching realized beta: if the prune of small-cap cyclicals does not bring beta below ~1.15 while excess return holds, the risk reduction is illusory. Pre-registered so this is judged without hindsight.

stance: Risk-aware quality flexi-cap: hold the diversified compounder core, lean modestly toward names with rising analyst revisions, prune the falling-revision laggards, and dial beta down from 1.15.
experience_note: Last round's 62 bets are all still ungraded (zero-day horizon), so there is nothing to learn from outcomes yet and I am deliberately not re-anchoring on hindsight. What I am watching to test THIS round's central bet: (1) does the ARM-confirmed ADD basket (Grasim, ONGC, LICI, Coforge, Muthoot, Lenskart, CreditAccess) beat the low-ARM EXIT basket (LT, HPCL, Adani Ports, SRF, Sumitomo, Max Financial) over the next 1-3 months? If the laggards I cut outperform the names I added, the ARM overlay added no value and I should down-weight it next round. (2) Does cutting high-beta cyclicals actually pull realized beta toward 1.0 without sacrificing the +6.6% historical excess return? If beta stays at 1.15, the trims were cosmetic. (3) Because z_mom and z_val were flat this round, I am pre-registering that this is a low-conviction, low-turnover rebalance — I should NOT expect a big active return either way, and a large active move (positive or negative) would tell me my read of "flat cross-section" was wrong.
